title: PEARL trial published: first long-term human RCT shows rapamycin slows muscle/bone aging without serious side effects
description: The Participatory Evaluation of Aging with Rapamycin for Longevity (PEARL) study reports that 48 weeks of 10 mg/week rapamycin preserved muscle mass in women and bone mineral content in men in adults 50-85 with no serious adverse events. This is the strongest long-term human safety dataset for the field's gold-standard geroprotector.

title: Stanfield-Kaeberlein rapamycin strength/endurance RCT launches — first read-out on 6 mg/week in older adults
description: A new BMC-registered double-blind placebo-controlled trial led by Brad Stanfield MD with Matt Kaeberlein enrolls 40 adults aged 65-80 on 6 mg/week sirolimus for 13 weeks measuring strength and endurance. The trial is the next major translational checkpoint for weekly low-dose rapamycin in healthy older adults.

title: Nature Signal Transduction & Targeted Therapy publishes 2026 master review of senolytics, senomorphics, senoreversion, and metabolic interventions
description: A comprehensive 2026 review (Nature) consolidates the entire anti-aging toolkit — D+Q, ABT263, fisetin, CAR-T senolytics, rapamycin and metformin as senomorphics, partial reprogramming, NAD precursors, urolithin A, spermidine — into one translational framework. The authors argue the field is pivoting to biomarker-guided combination interventions but long-term human trials remain the bottleneck.

title: Longevity Technology Week 23: Ōura Ring 5 launches with blood-pressure + GLP-1 insights; Elysium opens TELI longevity clinic
description: Ōura's Ring 5 is 40% smaller with week-long battery life and adds blood-pressure signals, breathing monitoring, and GLP-1 insights — pushing consumer wearables into clinical territory. Elysium Health concurrently launched The Elysium Longevity Institute (TELI), combining physician oversight with testing, supplements, prescriptions, and peptides. The two together signal the consumer/medical longevity stack is converging.

title: 60 Minutes profiles Dog Aging Project rapamycin trial — national NIH-funded RCT now dosing hundreds of dogs
description: CBS News' 60 Minutes profiles the Dog Aging Project's NIH-funded large-scale rapamycin RCT in pet dogs — over 50,000 dogs enrolled in the broader study, with hundreds in the active rapamycin vs placebo arm. This is the most public-facing translational readout the rapamycin field has ever received and a key bridge to human geroscience approvals.

title: Anti-Aging Supplement Rapamycin Slows Muscle and Bone Aging in First Long-Term Clinical Trial
description: PEARL trial (115 participants 50-85, 48 weeks) finds 10 mg/week rapamycin prevented muscle loss in women and bone loss in men with no severe adverse events.

title: Brad Stanfield and Matt Kaeberlein's new rapamycin clinical trial measures strength and endurance
description: New BMC-registered double-blind RCT testing 6 mg/week sirolimus vs placebo in 40 adults aged 65-80 for 13 weeks, with strength and endurance as primary endpoints.

title: 60 Minutes: Dog Aging Project rapamycin trial could unlock secrets for people
description: 60 Minutes profiles the NIH-funded Dog Aging Project rapamycin RCT — hundreds of dogs now dosed on rapamycin or placebo with the goal of confirming healthspan extension translates to mammals living human-aligned environments.

title: Nature review: insights into therapeutic strategies for aging and age-associated diseases
description: Sweeping 2026 review integrating senolytics, senomorphics, senoreversion, metabolic/dietary interventions, biomarkers, multiomics and AI into a unified translational geroscience framework.

title: Longevity Technology Roundup Week 23: brain interfaces, Ōura Ring 5, Elysium TELI clinic
description: Ōura Ring 5 (40% smaller, BP + breathing + GLP-1 insights); Ability Neurotech battery-free optical brain implant Netherlands approval; Elysium opens longevity clinic; sleep/AD-genetic-risk visual-memory study.

title: Longevity Technology Roundup Week 22: Sam Altman's $1.8B Retro, CRISPR & AI
description: Retro Biosciences reaches $1.8B valuation while advancing an Alzheimer's drug into human trials; Sunrise gets FDA clearance for at-home rechargeable sleep apnea diagnostic.

title: Kaeberlein AGE2026 Day 1 reactions: mTOR dominance, genomic stability via rapamycin
description: Kell et al. 2026 data shows low-dose rapamycin reduces p21 expression and γH2AX genomic instability markers in human T-cells — a new senomorphic mechanism distinct from autophagy; chemo co-administration potential highlighted.

title: Loss of kidney function correlates with progression of frailty
description: Large cohort analysis showing middle-aged and older adults with lower kidney function had higher baseline frailty index and faster frailty progression over time.

title: Sinclair receives Honorary Doctorate at UNIC; Life Biosciences first-in-human gene therapy active
description: Sinclair conferred Doctor of Philosophy Honoris Causa at University of Nicosia; UNIC confirms Life Biosciences' partial-reprogramming gene therapy has reached first-in-human trials (glaucoma, NAION).

title: Sinclair + Tally Health publish first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: Frontiers in Genetics review identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diet, caloric restriction, omega-3, multivitamin and emerging clinical therapies as interventions associated with favorable next-gen epigenetic clock changes (41 human studies).

title: UCSD: Popular GLP-1 drug may slow biological aging — Nature Communications RCT
description: 108-adult RCT shows semaglutide slowed DunedinPACE by 9% and PCGrimAge mortality clock relative to placebo over 32 weeks in HIV lipohypertrophy patients — first randomized human evidence GLP-1s influence aging biology.

title: Popular weight-loss drug found to slow biological aging in landmark human trial
description: Coverage of Corley et al. Nature Communications RCT showing semaglutide slows epigenetic aging across heart, brain, kidney, liver, blood, metabolic clocks.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C at $3.1B valuation
description: Founders Fund-led round (Thrive, Lilly Ventures, Friedman, Gross) brings the Brian Armstrong-founded cell reprogramming company to $3.1B ahead of its first liver age-reversal human trial.

title: 7 VCs funding longevity startups in 2026
description: Industry profile of the leading VC firms backing longevity, citing three converging forces: CRISPR therapies entering clinic, AI-driven drug discovery, and millionfold sequencing cost drop.

title: Axo Longevity to showcase preventive health at The Longevity Show, London Jun 26-27
description: Tobacco Dock event in London on 26-27 June 2026 will bring leading voices from longevity science, preventive healthcare, wellness, tech and investment — preview from Axo Longevity.

title: How METiS TechBio and Human Longevity use algorithms in cellular reprogramming
description: Profile of AI-enabled cellular reprogramming companies forecasting health risks and accelerating anti-aging therapeutic discovery.

title: Sirolimus now available at TrumpRx ($0.66/mg)
description: Generic sirolimus now listed on TrumpRx.gov at $0.66/mg — material pricing development for off-label longevity users on weekly rapamycin protocols.

title: Old antidepressant mianserin extends mouse lifespan 17% — calcium signaling implicated
description: Tetracyclic antidepressant mianserin extends median lifespan in mice by 17.5%, with researchers pointing to calcium homeostasis as a possible underlying aging clock mechanism.

title: Butyrate: the microbiome's anti-aging 'kill switch' for senescent cells
description: Discussion of new data on butyrate as a senolytic-like microbiome-derived metabolite that selectively clears senescent cells, with implications for fiber/SCFA-focused longevity diets.

title: Bryan Johnson publishes 40-point longevity learnings list
description: Johnson distills years of self-experimentation into a 40-point checklist covering sleep timing, sun exposure, avoiding added sugar, lifting heavy, oral hygiene, hearing protection, and pre-noon caffeine.

title: Bryan Johnson's peptide list updated 2026
description: Current Blueprint peptide protocol: topical 8-peptide Blueprint hair serum daily, 20-30g oral collagen + vitamin C, Tirzepatide + CJC-1295 DAC experimental stack; Cerebrolysin, Epithalon, Thymulin all dropped.

title: Peter Attia: The beginning of the end of atherosclerosis?
description: Attia outlines current tools (exercise, sleep, diet, statin-era meds) and emerging technologies that could move atherosclerosis from dominant cause of death to tractable condition.

title: Rapamycin in endometriosis and fertility: a new frontier
description: Clinical synthesis of Columbia VIBRANT and Li et al. Cell Reports Medicine RCT showing 1 mg/day rapamycin for 21-28 days raised IVF clinical pregnancy rate from 28% to 50% in poor responders.

title: D+Q senolytic linked to brain myelin damage in mice — UConn safety warning
description: UConn researchers report dasatinib + quercetin at standard longevity doses causes significant demyelination in corpus callosum white matter in young and old mice — urgent caution for human off-label use.

title: Olive leaf extract WOL improves skin aging markers in 50-person RCT
source: Functional Foods in Health and Disease
description: 12-week RCT in 50 Japanese adults aged 40-69 shows 250 mg/day water-soluble olive leaf extract (WOL) increased epidermal/dermal stem cell markers and improved skin-age scores most strongly in non-exercisers.
